Hi, I restored this original patch and added the changes to it. The other patchset is now abandoned, as I could not rebase it with master.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Good start.  Couple comments and questions below

- Kafka config handling is not consistent with rest of PRH config handling which done through CbsContentParser class
- Not sure if I missed in my review, where is the Configuration to toggle PRH msg reprocessing to be enabled/disabled? i.e how will this behavior controlled via helm configuration
- Based on proposal discussion earlier, thought SDK lib will be enhanced to support the kafka interfaces? If this is something planned to be addressed separetely, pls create a jira for tracking this work.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1
> 
> Good start.  Couple comments and questions below
> 
> - Kafka config handling is not consistent with rest of PRH config handling which done through CbsContentParser class
> - Not sure if I missed in my review, where is the Configuration to toggle PRH msg reprocessing to be enabled/disabled? i.e how will this behavior controlled via helm configuration
> - Based on proposal discussion earlier, thought SDK lib will be enhanced to support the kafka interfaces? If this is something planned to be addressed separetely, pls create a jira for tracking this work.

We did not use CbsContentParser since we assumed the CBS is not / will not be used anymore. Can we make this change for next release, as we are already on M3.

The PRH msg reprocessing is an optional mode that is implemented in a different Spring Profile. This profile can be activated if the Helm deployment template contains an environment variable: autoCommitDisabled: true. 
The current working version  which is in the default Spring profile is not affected by our changes.

The SDK changes will be addressed separately. The JIRA will be created.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > Patch Set 5:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1
> > > 
> > > Good start.  Couple comments and questions below
> > > 
> > > - Kafka config handling is not consistent with rest of PRH config handling which done through CbsContentParser class
> > > - Not sure if I missed in my review, where is the Configuration to toggle PRH msg reprocessing to be enabled/disabled? i.e how will this behavior controlled via helm configuration
> > > - Based on proposal discussion earlier, thought SDK lib will be enhanced to support the kafka interfaces? If this is something planned to be addressed separetely, pls create a jira for tracking this work.
> > 
> > We did not use CbsContentParser since we assumed the CBS is not / will not be used anymore. Can we make this change for next release, as we are already on M3.
> > 
> > The PRH msg reprocessing is an optional mode that is implemented in a different Spring Profile. This profile can be activated if the Helm deployment template contains an environment variable: - name: SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE
>           value: autoCommitDisabled
> > The current working version  which is in the default Spring profile is not affected by our changes.
> > 
> > The SDK changes will be addressed separately. The JIRA will be created.

For config handling it will be good to have consistency (as some config are retrieved through CBS lib and kafka config through ENV); CBS lib has provision to substitute property values from ENV settings - that could be used here and all config managed through CBS. This change can go for next release - pls create a jira and post here for reference.

Reg profile enable/disablment - Thanks for info/clarification, no further question here.

And for SDK changes - pls create separate jira for tracking this enhancement and reference it here.

2. DCAEGEN2-3365 - Use CBSContentParser for environment variables in PRH autoCommitDisabled mode.

> Has this been tested and verified in any way?
> The main objective is to wait to commit the offsets until the PNF is ready correct?
> How can this be verified?

We have tested it in our ONAP Kohn environment and verified that it works as expected i.e. commit offset only when PNF is found in AAI, and to avoid accumulation of too many events in Kafka, discard uncorrelated events older than a configurable age limit , set to default 1 day.

Thanks for clarifications, I'm okay to merge. Will wait another day/two to see if others have any other comments/questions.
